evals.prompt: I have an idea for an app that helps people find local mechanics they can trust. Like a mix between Yelp and Angi but specifically for auto repair. I've been ripped off twice and I know I'm not the only one.
evals.expected_output: Should start the intake interview, asking targeted follow-up questions about the idea, target market, founder background, business model preferences, etc. Should NOT jump straight to generating files.

evals.prompt: I want to build a B2B SaaS that uses AI to automate invoice reconciliation for mid-size companies (50-500 employees). I'm a former CFO with 10 years experience in corporate finance and my technical co-founder has built ML pipelines at Stripe. We have $80k in savings to bootstrap. We're targeting the US market first, specifically companies still using Excel for reconciliation. We think $500/mo per company is reasonable. Our biggest worry is that the big ERP players (SAP, Oracle) will just add this feature.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this is a detailed input, confirm understanding, ask fewer but more targeted follow-up questions, then move to brainstorming and research phases. Should produce the full file structure with substantive content grounded in web research.

evals.prompt: Ho un'idea per una piattaforma che connette piccoli produttori alimentari italiani direttamente con ristoranti in Europa. Tipo un marketplace B2B per prodotti artigianali. Sono un food consultant con 15 anni di esperienza.
evals.expected_output: Should detect Italian language and produce outputs in Italian. Should start the intake interview in Italian, asking relevant follow-ups about the food industry, regulatory considerations (EU food safety), logistics, etc.

evals.prompt: I want to validate a startup idea. It's a Chrome extension that uses AI to summarize and prioritize Slack messages for remote workers. I've already done some customer interviews - 23 out of 30 people said they'd pay for it. I don't have a technical background but I can invest about $20k. I want to know if this is worth pursuing or if I should move on.
evals.expected_output: Should focus heavily on validation given the user's explicit ask. Should acknowledge existing customer interview data and build on it. Should be honest about risks (competitive landscape with Slack's own AI features, dependency on Slack's API, non-technical solo founder). The scorecard should reflect real analysis, not cheerleading.
Should detect the PROGRESS.md file, read it, and resume from the last incomplete phase. Should clearly communicate what was already completed and what comes next.
